title: Politically Incorrect Paper of the Day: The US Racial Wealth Gap - Marginal REVOLUTION
gist: Tabarrok highlights Brian Marein's data-driven challenge to the slavery-origins explanation of the black-white wealth gap: penniless European immigrants (1850-1924) converged rapidly with native whites, implying the racial gap reflects income/savings differences rather than persistent initial conditions.

insights.claim: Derenoncourt et al. (QJE) attribute today's black-white wealth gap to differences in initial conditions over 150 years ago, i.e. slavery, but mass European immigration of wealthless whites who caught up quickly is a glaring objection.
insights.speaker: Alex Tabarrok
insights.so_what: Frames a live academic dispute over the mechanism of persistent inequality — bears on which policy levers (wealth transfers vs. income) are credible.
insights.quote: millions of whites immigrated to the United States with essentially no wealth and yet they caught up to the “heritage” whites quite quickly
insights.locator: But there is an important, and glaring objection

insights.claim: Marein documents that 30 million European immigrants (1850-1924) arrived with substantial wealth deficits yet their descendants closed those deficits by the late 20th century, showing rapid intraracial convergence faster than cross-racial convergence.
insights.speaker: Alex Tabarrok
insights.so_what: Evidence that large wealth gaps do not mechanically persist when groups have comparable opportunity — reframes the inequality debate around access and income.
insights.quote: large wealth disparities do not mechanically persist when groups have access to comparable economic opportunities
insights.locator: These findings demonstrate that large wealth disparities

insights.claim: If initial conditions don't explain the wealth gap, the most likely remaining explanation is an income and/or savings gap.
insights.speaker: Alex Tabarrok
insights.so_what: Shifts the explanatory burden to current income/savings dynamics rather than historical wealth transmission.
insights.quote: the most likely explanation is an income and/or savings gaps
insights.locator: If initial conditions don’t explain the wealth gap
